title: Composite safety films
abstract: A composite safety film may include a base layer and a protective layer. The base layer may include a PET material. The protective layer may include a fluoropolymer and overly the base layer. The protective layer may further be the outermost layer of the safety film. The protective layer may have an ultra violet light absorption of at least about 85%. The composite safety film may have a Yellowness UVI Weatherability of not greater than about 3 after 3000 hours of ultra violet light irradiation, a Haze UVI Weatherability of not greater than 5% after 3000 hours of ultra violet light irradiation, or a Tensile Strength UVI Weatherability of not greater than about 25% after 3000 hours of ultra violet light irradiation.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A composite safety film comprising: a base layer; and a protective layer comprising a fluoropolymer overlying the base layer, wherein the protective layer is the outermost layer of the composite safety film. 2. A composite safety film comprising: a base layer comprising a PET material; and a protective layer comprising a fluoropolymer overlying the base layer, wherein the protective layer is the outermost layer of the safety film; wherein the protective layer comprises an ultra violet light absorption of at least about 85%; and wherein the composite safety film has a Yellowness UVI Weatherability of not greater than about 3 after 3000 hours of ultra violet light irradiation, a Haze UVI Weatherability of not greater than 5% after 3000 hours of ultra violet light irradiation, or a Tensile Strength UVI Weatherability of not greater than about 25% after 3000 hours of ultra violet light irradiation. 3. A method of forming a composite safety film comprising: providing a base layer comprising a PET material; and forming a protective layer comprising a fluoropolymer over die base layer, wherein the protective layer is the outermost layer of the composite safety film; wherein the protective layer comprises an ultra violet light absorption of at least about 85%; and wherein the composite safety film has a Yellowness UVI Weatherability of not greater than about 3 after 3000 hours of ultra violet light irradiation. 4. The composite safety film or method of any one of claims 1, 2, and 3, wherein the protective layer comprises a fluoropolymer layer and wherein the fluoropolymer layer is the outmost layer of the composite safety film. 5. The composite safety film or method of any one of claims 1, 2, and 3, wherein the protective layer comprises a UV barrier layer and wherein the fluoropolymer layer overlies the UV barrier layer. 6. The composite safety film or method of claim S, wherein the UV barrier layer comprises PET, modified PMMA, acrylic, EVA, PVC, PC or a combination thereof. 7. The composite safety film or method of claim 5, wherein the UV barrier layer comprises a UV absorber/stabilizer component. 8. The composite safety film or method of claim 7, wherein the UV absorber layer comprise a content of the UV absorber/stabilizer component of at least about 0.1 wt.% and not greater than about 10 wt.%. 9. The composite safety film or method of any one of claims 1, 2, and 3, wherein the protective layer has an ultra violet light absorption of at least about 85%. 10. The composite safety film or method of claim 5, wherein the UV barrier layer has an ultra violet light absorption of at least about 85%. 11. The composite safety film or method of any one of claims 1, 2, and 3, wherein the safety film has an ultra violet light absorption of at least about 85%. 12. The composite safety film or method of any one of claims 1, 2, and 3, wherein the protective layer comprises a surface energy of at least about 18 dyne/cm and not greater than about 40 dyne/cm 13. The composite safety film or method of claim 4, wherein the fluoropolymer layer comprises PVDF, PVF, ETFE, FEP, PFA, THV, ECTFE or PCTFE. 14. The composite safety film or method of any one of claims 1, 2, and 3, wherein the protective layer has a thickness of at least about 25 microns and not greater than about 150 microns. 15. The composite safety film or method of any one of claims 1, 2, and 3, wherein the fluoropolymer layer has a thickness of at least about 2 microns and not greater than about 50 microns.
